Hide Ball: Brain Teaser Game

Hide Ball is a physics-based puzzle game where you must protect good balls (green) from monster balls (red). Use your limited moves to strategically place blocks and barriers in the environment. After you've used all your moves, the monster balls activate and seek out the good balls. Keep all good balls safe for 5 seconds to win the level!

Arrow Keys / WASD: Move selected block | Space: Select/Deselect block | Enter: Start game | Esc: Pause/Unpause | R: Restart to menu

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic block movement and selection mechanics. This test verifies that players can select movable blocks using Space and move them around the arena using arrow keys or WASD. It also checks that moves are properly counted and the game transitions to the monster activation phase when moves run out.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Press Space to select the first movable block, then use arrow keys to move it right and down to create a barrier. Press Space again to deselect, then select another block and move it to a different position. Continue until all moves are exhausted, allowing the game to transition to monster activation phase.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The test is successful if blocks can be selected and moved smoothly, the move counter decrements with each movement, and the game automatically transitions to the monster activation phase when moves reach zero. The selected block should be visually highlighted, and movement should feel responsive.

Use all available moves to build walls that physically block the monster balls' paths. Wait for the monster activation phase to complete, ensuring monsters cannot reach the good balls.
test_2_ModeBtn.expected: The test is successful if the game detects that all good balls remain untouched after the 5-second monster activation phase and transitions to the GAME_OVER_WIN state. The victory screen should display with the option to proceed to the next level or restart. No collisions between monster balls and good balls should occur.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing lose condition scenario. This test verifies that when a monster ball touches a good ball during the activation phase, the game correctly detects the collision and transitions to the game over lose state immediately.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Intentionally avoid placing any blocks or place them ineffectively so that monster balls have clear paths to good balls. Use minimal moves or place blocks away from the danger zones. Allow the monster activation phase to proceed without proper protection.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: The test is successful if the game immediately detects when a monster ball collides with a good ball and transitions to GAME_OVER_LOSE state. The lose screen should display showing which good ball was lost and offering the restart option.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing pause functionality during gameplay. This test ensures that pressing ESC during the PLAYING phase properly pauses all physics simulation, monster movement, and game logic while maintaining the current game state for resumption.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Start a level and make a few moves to place blocks. Press ESC to pause the game during the move phase. Verify the pause state, then press ESC again to unpause. Make more moves, then pause during the monster activation phase to ensure physics properly stops.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: The test is successful if the game properly freezes all movement and physics when paused, displays a pause overlay, and seamlessly resumes from the exact same state when unpaused. Monster balls should not move during pause, and the timer should not count down.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing multiple level progression. This test verifies that after winning a level, the game properly loads the next level with increased difficulty, resets the move counter, and maintains proper game state transitions between levels.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Complete the first level by successfully protecting all good balls. After the win screen appears, press Enter to proceed to the next level. Verify that the new level loads with different configurations, more challenging layouts, or additional obstacles.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: The test is successful if the game seamlessly transitions to the next level after a win, properly resets all game state variables (moves, timer, ball positions), and presents a new level configuration. Level number should increment and difficulty should increase appropriately.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing block collision physics with balls. This test verifies that movable blocks properly interact with both good balls and monster balls, creating realistic physical barriers that balls cannot pass through or push aside easily.
TEST_6.strategy_description: Place blocks directly in the path of monster balls so they must collide with the blocks during the activation phase. Position blocks close to good balls to test if they provide effective protection. Observe how balls bounce off or interact with the placed blocks.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: The test is successful if blocks create solid barriers that monster balls cannot easily penetrate. Balls should bounce off blocks realistically based on Matter.js physics. Blocks should remain stable when hit by balls and not move significantly, providing reliable protection.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing edge cases and boundary conditions. This test verifies that balls and blocks properly interact with arena boundaries, that the game handles situations where balls get stuck, and that the timer properly counts down during monster activation phase.
TEST_7.strategy_description: Try placing blocks at the edges of the arena near walls. Attempt to move blocks outside the playable area. Let monster balls reach the boundaries to see how they behave. Monitor the activation timer to ensure it counts down correctly from 5 seconds.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: The test is successful if blocks cannot be moved outside the arena boundaries, balls properly bounce off walls, the activation timer counts down accurately and triggers the win/lose condition at exactly 5 seconds, and no entities escape the playable area or behave unexpectedly.
